questions.question: 1 (a) • Switch on. • Place the sliding contact C on the resistance wire at a distance l = 10.0 cm from B. • Measure, and record in Table 2.1, the potential difference V across the length l of the resistance wire. • Measure, and record in Table 2.1, the current I in the circuit. • Repeat the procedure using l = 30.0 cm, 50.0 cm, 70.0 cm and 90.0 cm. • Switch off. Table 2.

Table 2.

statement to match results j ustification to match statement and including clear reference with appropriate numbers to results temperature of hot wate r MP1 diagram showing strip clamped to bench with majority overhanging MP2 means to measure bending, e.g. vertical metre rule at end of strip MP3 add load at / near end of strip and measure the amount of depression MP4 repeat with other strips MP5 variables any one from: all strips to have same width / thickness / profile use of same load(s) allowance for unloaded depression MP6 table with columns for material, load and depression with correct units MP7 strip that bends most with same load is most bendy / alternative wording
